name: MapReduce
def: A model for processing huge datasets by a parallel map step followed by a grouped reduce step.
problem: Process petabyte-scale data across a cluster with automatic parallelism and fault tolerance.
mechanism: Map emits key-value pairs per record in parallel; the framework shuffles/groups by key; reduce aggregates each group. The runtime handles distribution, retries and data locality.
failures: Forcing iterative ML into many MR passes
whenNot: Low-latency, iterative, or graph workloads (use Spark/Flink)
myths: "MapReduce is the only way to do big data" — modern engines generalise and outperform it

complexity.Note: Scales ~linearly with cluster size; shuffle is the bottleneck
